{"query":"Nintendo Switch 2 Octopath Traveler 1 2 enhanced visuals performance comparison resolution frame rate","model":"mako","response_time_ms":925,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.rpgsite.net/feature/20905-octopath-traveler-i-ii-nintendo-switch-2-port-review-impressions-graphics-performance-frame-rate","title":"Octopath Traveler I and II for Switch 2 deliver solid improvements over the Switch 1 versions | RPG Site","content":"4 days ago — Octopath Traveler and Octopath Traveler II targeted 30fps on Switch 1 and had frame pacing issues. The Switch 2 versions target 60fps in both ...Read more
Today, Square Enix announced and released both Octopath Traveler and Octopath Traveler II on Switch 2 in Japan with the Western releases set for October 1.
For some background, both games ran at a sub-native resolution (below 720p handheld and below 1080p docked) on Switch 1.
The PS4 version has PS4 Pro support, but the PS5 versions of Octopath Traveler and Octopath Traveler II do not have PS5 Pro support.
The Switch 2 offering this big visual improvement over Switch 1 and a 60fps target means I'd likely play this over other platforms just to have the flexibility of playing on the go when needed, but I hope the ports can be improved a bit leading into the Western release this October.
While the visual upgrade is very noticeable over Switch 1, it is more notable for delivering that at twice the frame rate target.
Performance is massively improved over the Switch 1 versions with their 30fps cap, but it isn't perfect.
I noticed some dips early on when playing docked.
Not only do both Octopath Traveler and Octopath Traveler II now target 60fps, but they also deliver improved visuals handheld while aiming for 60fps.
I used the accessibility zooming and took offscreen handheld photos with my phone to show the difference since Switch 1 game screenshots are capped at 720p in boost mode regardless of the actual resolution.
Octopath Traveler and Octopath Traveler II are now available on PS5, PS4, Switch 2 (in Japan), Switch, Xbox, and PC platforms."},{"url":"https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/07/octopath-traveler-1-and-2-announced-for-switch-2-with-improved-performance-and-visuals","title":"Octopath Traveler 1 & 2 Announced For Switch 2 With Improved Performance And Visuals | Nintendo Life","content":"4 days ago — on 1st October 2026. for £49.99 / $59.99 each, We're guessing a slight resolution bump along with 60fps gameplay is in order, but does that ...
Square Enix has announced Octopath Traveler and Octopath Traveler 2 for the Switch 2, bringing two modern RPG classics to Nintendo's latest console with improved visuals and performance.
Although they're out now in Japan, a western release is due to follow on 1st October 2026.
"There is also a Nintendo Switch™ version of this game.
There are no plans to sell an upgrade pack to upgrade the Nintendo Switch™ version to the Nintendo Switch™ 2 version.
With that in mind, this will likely be a tough sell for current owners.
Specifics regarding how the visuals and performance have been improved have not been provided at this time, but given the games' availability in Japan, it's likely we'll find out soon.
It's just disappointing that publishers seem so hesitant to provide any benefits to fans who have already pledged their support on the Switch."},{"url":"https://ninfora.com/forums/index.php?/topic/20083-octopath-traveler-iii-comes-to-switch-2-october-1st/","title":"Octopath Traveler I&II comes to Switch 2 - October 1st","date":"4 days ago","content":"4 days ago — improved resolution and framerate.
